coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of what a foundation model is and how it is pretrained on large corpora
Elementary grasp of tokenization and how text becomes model inputs
Able to run steps of a data-preprocessing pipeline under supervision
Basic understanding of the transformer architecture and next-token prediction
Able to inspect and clean a slice of a training dataset following guidance
Elementary practice of reading training loss and basic metrics
Basic understanding of why scale matters for foundation models
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with Python and distributed data processing
Familiar with running jobs on a GPU cluster
Elementary notion of probability and language modeling
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can run pipeline steps with detailed guidance
Needs 3-6 months to become productive

coreCompetencies: Able to build stages of a data pipeline for filtering and deduplicating text
Grasp of tokenizer training and vocabulary construction
Able to run data-parallel pretraining jobs following an established recipe
Practical measurement of dataset quality and contamination
Able to monitor a training run for loss spikes and divergence
Grasp of checkpointing and resuming long training jobs
Familiar with computing evaluation metrics such as perplexity on held-out data
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with distributed data tools such as Spark or Ray
Familiar with dataset formats and streaming large corpora
Basic practice of the transformer forward and backward pass
indicators: Contributes to pretraining pipelines with supervision
Resolves medium-complexity issues in data and training jobs
Actively participates in code and data reviews

coreCompetencies: Proficient at building large-scale deduplication and quality-filtering pipelines
Able to configure tensor and data parallelism for a pretraining run
Able to diagnose and recover from training instability such as loss spikes
Solid command of tokenization choices and their effect on model quality
Able to implement robust checkpointing and resumption for long runs
Grasp of scaling laws and how compute, data and parameters relate
Able to build evaluation suites that measure base-model capabilities
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of data-contamination detection against evaluation benchmarks
Able to implement supervised fine-tuning on top of a base model
Basic practice of collective communication in distributed training
indicators: Completes complex pretraining engineering tasks with minimal supervision
Identifies and resolves failures in data and training pipelines
Actively contributes to pretraining-recipe design

coreCompetencies: Able to design data pipelines that curate trillions of tokens with dedup and filtering
Proficient at combining tensor, pipeline and data parallelism for large-model training
Able to hold training stable through careful initialization and learning-rate control
Solid command of checkpointing, fault tolerance and elastic training at scale
Able to use scaling laws to plan compute-optimal model and data sizes
Proficient with base-model evaluation across capability and safety benchmarks
Able to implement alignment methods such as RLHF or DPO on a base model
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of data governance including licensing and provenance of training data
Able to profile and optimize throughput of large distributed training
Grasp of tokenizer trade-offs across languages and domains
indicators: Leads development of critical pretraining components
Effectively mentors junior engineers
Makes informed decisions on pretraining-system design

coreCompetencies: Expert at designing pretraining systems that run stably across thousands of GPUs
Able to architect data pipelines that filter, dedup and tokenize web-scale corpora
Proficient with parallelism strategies tuned to model shape and cluster topology
Able to design training recipes informed by scaling-law experiments
Solid command of diagnosing and preventing instability in large training runs
Able to build alignment pipelines with RLHF, DPO and reward modeling
Expert at base-model evaluation including capability, robustness and safety
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of data provenance, licensing and safety filtering at scale
Able to design compute-efficient ablations before full-scale runs
Grasp of red teaming and safety evaluation of base models
indicators: Designs pretraining systems for full model-training programs
Defines technical standards for data and training pipelines
Leads cross-team initiatives on pretraining infrastructure

coreCompetencies: Able to architect the end-to-end pretraining stack from data to aligned model
Expert at running training programs reliably across large GPU clusters
Proficient with data-curation strategy including quality, diversity and safety
Able to design systems resilient to hardware failure during month-long runs
Solid command of scaling laws applied to planning frontier training budgets
Able to lead adoption of new parallelism and training techniques across the org
Expert at reliability engineering for large-scale distributed pretraining
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of model safety evaluation and mitigation before release
Able to design shared data and evaluation infrastructure for model training
Grasp of the compute economics of frontier-model training
indicators: Architects pretraining infrastructure for multiple model efforts
Influences organizational decisions on model-training strategy
Represents the company technically in the foundation-model domain

coreCompetencies: Provides technical leadership for foundation-model training programs across an organization
Able to design pretraining architectures for successive generations of models
Expert at strategies for extracting maximum capability per unit of training compute
Solid command of data strategy spanning acquisition, curation, safety and governance
Able to define multi-year roadmaps for model training and alignment
Deep grasp of the cost, risk and capability trade-offs of frontier training
Expert at building and scaling foundation-model engineering teams
complementaryCompetencies: Able to evaluate emerging architectures and training methods for adoption
Able to guide model safety and release decisions with stakeholders
Aware of trends in scaling, data and alignment research
indicators: Defines organizational strategy for model pretraining
Recognized as a technical authority in foundation-model training
Speaks at machine-learning conferences

coreCompetencies: Sets the strategic vision for the organization's foundation-model training program
Able to lead major shifts in how models are trained, aligned and evaluated
Expert at technical due diligence for model-training investment and compute
Drives organizational change toward safe, efficient frontier training
Able to build and lead foundation-model engineering organizations
Deep grasp of the impact of data, scale and alignment decisions on model quality
Expert at stakeholder relations around model capability and safety risk
complementaryCompetencies: Able to influence industry practice on responsible model training
Able to guide investment in compute for model-training programs
Aware of the frontier-model and accelerator landscape
indicators: Impacts the technical direction of foundation-model training in the field
Advises leadership on model-training and safety strategy
Mentors senior technical leaders

coreCompetencies: Provides executive leadership for foundation-model training across the organization
Able to align model-training strategy with business and safety commitments
Expert at managing very large budgets for training compute and data
Commands stakeholder and board communication on model training and safety
Able to lead the organization's roadmap for successive model generations
Deep grasp of risk management for frontier-model capability and release
Expert at building competitive advantage through foundation-model capability
complementaryCompetencies: Able to shape organizational policy on model safety and responsible scaling
Able to build partnerships for compute, data and evaluation
Aware of the economics of frontier-model programs
indicators: Defines and executes the foundation-model technology vision
Accountable for model-training strategy, budget and safety
Serves as the public voice of the company in foundation-model development
